functions.name: soxs.utils.downsample
documentation:
Downsample a 2D array by averaging over *factor* pixels in each axis.
Crops upper edge if the shape is not a multiple of factor.

This code is pure numpy and should be fast.

keywords:
    estimator - default to mean.  You can downsample by summing or
        something else if you want a different estimator
        (e.g., downsampling error: you want to sum & divide by sqrt(n))

functions.name: soxs.instrument_registry.add_instrument_to_registry
documentation:
Add an instrument specification to the registry, contained
in either a dictionary or a JSON file.

The *inst_spec* must have the structure as shown below.
The order is not important. If you use a JSON file, the
structure is the same, but the file cannot include comments,
and use "null" instead of "None", and "true" or "false"
instead of "True" or "False".

For the "chips" entry, "None" means no chips and the detector
field of view is a single square. If you want to have multiple
chips, they must be specified in a format described in the
online documentation.

```pycon
>>> {
...     "name": "lynx_hdxi", # The short name of the instrument
...     "arf": "xrs_hdxi_3x10.arf", # The file containing the ARF
...     "rmf": "xrs_hdxi.rmf", # The file containing the RMF
...     "bkgnd": ["lynx_hdxi_particle_bkgnd.pha", 1.0], # The name of the particle background file and the area of extraction
...     "fov": 20.0, # The field of view in arcminutes
...     "focal_length": 10.0, # The focal length in meters
...     "num_pixels": 4096, # The number of pixels on a side in the FOV
...     "dither": True, # Whether to dither the instrument
...     "psf": ["image", "chandra_psf.fits", 6], # The type of PSF and associated parameters
...     "chips": [["Box", 0, 0, 4096, 4096]], # The specification for the chips
...     "aimpt_coords": [0.0, 0.0], # The detector coordinates of the aimpoint
...     "imaging": True # Whether this is an imaging instrument
...     "grating": False # Whether this is a grating instrument
... }
```

functions.name: soxs.mosaic.make_mosaic_events
documentation:
Observe a source from many different pointings.

# Parameters

pointing_list

: Either a list of tuples or a two-column ASCII table, containing
  RA and Dec pointings for each mock observation.

input_source

: The path to the SIMPUT catalog file which contains the input
  source(s).

out_prefix

: The prefix for the event files which will be generated.

exp_time

: The exposure time in seconds.

instrument

: The name of the instrument to use, which picks an instrument
  specification from the instrument registry.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file with the same name.
  Default: False

instr_bkgnd

: Whether to include the instrumental/particle background.
  Default: True

foreground

: Whether to include the local foreground.
  Default: True

ptsrc_bkgnd

: Whether to include the point-source background.
  Default: True

bkgnd_file

: If set, backgrounds will be loaded from this file and not generated
  on the fly. Default: None

no_dither

: If True, turn off dithering entirely. Default: False

dither_params

: The parameters to use to control the size and period of the dither
  pattern. The first two numbers are the dither amplitude in x and y
  detector coordinates in arcseconds, and the second two numbers are
  the dither period in x and y detector coordinates in seconds.
  Default: [8.0, 8.0, 1000.0, 707.0].

subpixel_res: boolean, optional

: If True, event positions are not randomized within the pixels
  within which they are detected. Default: False

aimpt_shift

: A two-float array-like object which shifts the aimpoint on the
  detector from the nominal position. Units are in arcseconds.
  Default: None, which results in no shift from the nominal aimpoint.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

functions.name: soxs.mosaic.make_mosaic_image
documentation:
Make a single FITS image from a grid of observations. Optionally,
an exposure map can be computed and a flux image may be generated.

# Parameters

evtfile_list

: The ASCII table produced by {meth}`~soxs.grid.observe_grid_source`
  containing the information about the event files and their
  locations on the sky.

image_file

: The name of the FITS image file to be written. This name will
  also be used for the exposure map, event, and flux files if they are
  written.

evt_file

: The name of the mosaicked FITS event file to be written, if desired. Default
  is None, which writes no file.

emin

: The minimum energy of the photons to put in the image, in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy of the photons to put in the image, in keV.

reblock

: Supply an integer power of 2 here to make an exposure map
  with a different binning. Default: 1

use_expmap

: Whether to use (and potentially generate) an exposure map
  and a flux map. Default: False

expmap_energy

: The energy in keV to use when computing the exposure map, or
  a set of energies to be used with the *weights* parameter. If
  providing a set, it must be in keV.

expmap_weights

: The weights to use with a set of energies given in the
  *energy* parameter. Used to create a more accurate exposure
  map weighted by a range of energies. Default: None

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file with the same name.
  Default: False

functions.name: soxs.events.make_exposure_map
documentation:
Make an exposure map for a SOXS event file, and optionally write
an aspect solution file. The exposure map will be created by
binning an aspect histogram over the range of the aspect solution.

# Parameters

event_file

: The path to the event file to use for making the exposure map.

expmap_file

: The path to write the exposure map file to.

energy

: The energy in keV to use when computing the exposure map, or
  a set of energies to be used with the *weights* parameter. If
  providing a set, it must be in keV.

weights

: The weights to use with a set of energies given in the
  *energy* parameter. Used to create a more accurate exposure
  map weighted by a range of energies. Default: None

asol_file

: The path to write the aspect solution file to, if desired.
  Default: None

normalize

: If True, the exposure map will be divided by the exposure time
  so that the map's units are cm\*\*2. Default: True

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file. Default: False

reblock

: Supply an integer power of 2 here to make an exposure map
  with a different binning. Default: 1

nhistx

: The number of bins in the aspect histogram in the DETX
  direction. Default: 16

nhisty

: The number of bins in the aspect histogram in the DETY
  direction. Default: 16

order

: The interpolation order to use when making the exposure map.
  Default: 1

functions.name: soxs.events.filter_events
documentation:
# Parameters

evtfile

: The input events file to be read in.

newfile

: The new event file that will be written.

region

: The region(s) to be used for the filtering. Default: None

emin

: The minimum energy of the events to be included, in keV.
  Default is the lowest energy available.

emax

: The maximum energy of the events to be included, in keV.

tmin

: The minimum energy of the events to be included, in seconds.

: The maximum energy of the events to be included, in seconds.

format

: The file format specifier for the region, if supplied. "ds9",

exclude

  included. Default: False

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file with the
  same name. Default: False

functions.name: soxs.events.write_spectrum
documentation:
Bin event energies into a spectrum and write it to
a FITS binary table. Does not do any grouping of
channels, and will automatically determine PI or PHA.
Optionally allows filtering based on time, energy, or
spatial region.

# Parameters

evtfile

: The name of the event file to read the events from.

specfile

: The name of the spectrum file to be written.

region

: The region(s) to be used for the filtering. Default: None

format

: The file format specifier for the region. "ds9",
  "crtf", "fits", etc. Default: "ds9"

exclude

: If True, the events in a specified *region* will be excluded
  instead of included in the spectrum. Default: False

emin

: The minimum energy of the events to be included, in keV.
  Default is the lowest energy available.

emax

: The maximum energy of the events to be included, in keV.
  Default is the highest energy available.

tmin

: The minimum energy of the events to be included, in seconds.
  Default is the earliest time available.

tmax

: The maximum energy of the events to be included, in seconds.
  Default is the latest time available.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file with
  the same name. Default: False

functions.name: soxs.events.write_radial_profile
documentation:
Bin up events into a radial profile and write them to a FITS
table.

# Parameters

evt_file

: Input event file.

out_file

: The output file to write the profile to.

ctr

: The central coordinate of the profile. Can either be in
  celestial coordinates (the default) or "physical" pixel
  coordinates. If the former, the `ctr_type` keyword
  argument must be explicity set to "physical".

rmin

: The minimum radius of the profile, in arcseconds.

rmax

: The maximum radius of the profile, in arcseconds.

nbins

: The number of bins in the profile.

ctr_type

: The type of center coordinate. Either "celestial" for
  (RA, Dec) coordinates (the default), or "physical" for
  pixel coordinates.

emin

: The minimum energy of the events to be binned in keV.
  Default is the lowest energy available.

emax

: The maximum energy of the events to be binned in keV.
  Default is the highest energy available.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file with the
  same name. Default: False

expmap_file

: Supply an exposure map file to determine fluxes.
  Default: None

functions.name: soxs.events.make_image
documentation:
Generate an image by binning X-ray counts.

# Parameters

evt_file

: The name of the input event file to read.

coord_type

: The type of coordinate to bin into an image.
  Can be "sky" or "det". Default: "sky"

emin

: The minimum energy of the photons to put in the image, in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy of the photons to put in the image, in keV.

tmin

: The minimum energy of the events to be included, in seconds.
  Default is the earliest time available.

tmax

: The maximum energy of the events to be included, in seconds.
  Default is the latest time available.

bands

: A list of energy bands to restrict the counts used to make the
  image, in the form of [(emin1, emax1), (emin2, emax2), ...].
  Used as an alternative to emin and emax. Default: None

expmap_file

: Supply an exposure map file to divide this image by
  to get a flux map. Default: None

reblock

: Change this value to reblock the image to larger
  or small pixel sizes. Only supported for
  sky coordinates. Default: 1

bands

: A list of energy bands to restrict the counts used to make the

functions.name: soxs.events.fill_regions
documentation:
Fill regions which have been removed from an image (e.g. by
wavdetect) with a Poisson distribution of counts, either
from a single background region, a number of background regions
equal to the number of source regions, or from a single background
value.

Parameters
----------
in_img : string
    The file path to the input image.
out_img : string
    The file path to the image to be written with the filled
    regions.
src_reg : string, Region, or Regions
    The region(s) which will be filled.
bkg_val : float, string, Region, or Regions
    The background value(s) to use for the Poisson distribution.
    Can be a single value, a region, or a list of regions.
median : boolean
    If True, then the median value of the counts within the
    region will be used as the mean of the Poisson distribution
overwrite : boolean, optional
    Whether to overwrite an existing file with
    the same name. Default: False
format : string, optional
    The file format specifier for the region. "ds9",
    "crtf", "fits", etc. Default: "ds9"

functions.name: soxs.events.plot_spectrum
documentation:
Make a quick Matplotlib plot of a convolved spectrum
from a file. A Matplotlib figure and axis is returned.

# Parameters

specfile

: The file to be opened for plotting.

plot_energy

: Whether to plot in energy or channel space. Default is
  to plot in energy, unless the RMF for the spectrum
  cannot be found.

ebins

: If set, these are the energy bin edges in which the spectrum
  will be binned. If not set, the counts will be binned according
  to channel. Default: None

lw

: The width of the lines in the plots. Default: 2.0 px.

xmin

: The left-most energy (in keV) or channel to plot. Default is the
  minimum value in the spectrum.

xmax

: The right-most energy (in keV) or channel to plot. Default is the
  maximum value in the spectrum.

ymin

: The lower extent of the y-axis. By default it is set automatically.

ymax

: The upper extent of the y-axis. By default it is set automatically.

xscale

: The scaling of the x-axis of the plot. Default: "log"

yscale

: The scaling of the y-axis of the plot. Default: "log"

label

: The label of the spectrum. Default: None

fontsize

: Font size for labels and axes. Default: 18

fig

: A Figure instance to plot in. Default: None, one will be
  created if not provided.

ax

: An Axes instance to plot in. Default: None, one will be
  created if not provided.

plot_counts

: If set to True, the counts instead of the count rate will
  be plotted. Default: False

noerr

: If True, the spectrum will be plotted without errorbars. This
  will always be the case if the spectrum is not noisy.
  Default: False

plot_used

: If set to True, only the bins which contain more than 0
  counts will be plotted. Default: False

# Returns

A tuple of the {class}`~matplotlib.figure.Figure` and the
{class}`~matplotlib.axes.Axes` objects.

functions.name: soxs.events.plot_image
documentation:
Plot a FITS image created by SOXS using Matplotlib.

# Parameters

img_file

: The on-disk FITS image to plot.

hdu

: The image extension to plot. Default is "IMAGE"

stretch

: The stretch to apply to the colorbar scale. Options are "linear",
  "log", and "sqrt". Default: "linear"

vmin

: The minimum value of the colorbar. If not set, it will be the minimum
  value in the image.

vmax

: The maximum value of the colorbar. If not set, it will be the maximum
  value in the image.

facecolor

: The color of zero-valued pixels. Default: "black"

center

: A 2-element object giving an (RA, Dec) coordinate for the center
  in degrees. If not set, the reference pixel of the image (usually
  the center) is used.

width

: The width of the image in degrees. If not set, the width of the
  entire image will be used.

figsize

: A 2-tuple giving the size of the image in inches, e.g. (12, 15).
  Default: (10,10)

cmap

: The colormap to be used. If not set, the default Matplotlib
  colormap will be used.

# Returns

A tuple of the {class}`~matplotlib.figure.Figure` and the
{class}`~matplotlib.axes.Axes` objects.

functions.name: soxs.thermal_spectra.download_spectrum_tables
documentation:
Download thermal model spectrum tables used for the various
models supported by SOXS.

Parameters
----------
model : string
    Model string to specify which model files to download.
    Options are: "cie", "igm", "apec", "spex", "mekal"
model_vers : string
    The version of the model to download. If not specified,

methods.name: soxs.thermal_spectra.CIEGenerator.get_spectrum
documentation:
Get a thermal emission spectrum assuming CIE.

# Parameters

kT

: The temperature in keV.

abund

: The metal abundance in solar units.

redshift

: The redshift.

norm

: The normalization of the model, in the standard
  Xspec units of 1.0e-14\*EM/(4\*pi\*(1+z)\*\*2\*D_A\*\*2).

velocity

: The velocity broadening parameter, in units of
  km/s. Default: 0.0

elem_abund

: A dictionary of elemental abundances in solar
  units to vary freely of the abund parameter, e.g.
  {"O": 0.4, "N": 0.3, "He": 0.9}. Default: None

methods.name: soxs.thermal_spectra.CIEGenerator.get_nei_spectrum
documentation:
Get a thermal emission spectrum assuming NEI.

# Parameters

kT

: The temperature in keV.

elem_abund

: A dictionary of ionization state abundances in solar
  units to vary freely of the abund parameter, e.g.
  {"O^1": 0.4, "O^4": 0.6, "N^2": 0.7} Default: None

redshift

: The redshift.

norm

: The normalization of the model, in the standard
  Xspec units of 1.0e-14\*EM/(4\*pi\*(1+z)\*\*2\*D_A\*\*2).

velocity

: The velocity broadening parameter, in units of
  km/s. Default: 0.0

classes.name: soxs.thermal_spectra.ApecGenerator
documentation:
Create spectra assuming a thermal plasma emission model
in collisional ionization equilibrium from the
AtomDB APEC tables available at <http://www.atomdb.org>.
This code borrows heavily from Python routines used to
read the APEC tables developed by Adam Foster at the
CfA (<mailto:afoster@cfa.harvard.edu>).

# Parameters

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectral model.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectral model.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectral model.

binscale

: The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
  Default: "linear"

var_elem

: The names of elements to allow to vary freely
  from the single abundance parameter. These must be strings like
  ["O", "N", "He"], or if nei=True they must be elements with
  ionization states, e.g. ["O^1", "O^2", "N^4"]. Default:
  None

apec_root

: The directory root where the APEC model files
  are stored. If not provided, the default is to
  grab them from the tables stored with SOXS.

apec_vers

: The version identifier string for the APEC files. Default is
  set in the SOXS configuration file, the default for which is
  "3.0.9".

broadening

: Whether the spectral lines should be thermally and velocity
  broadened. Default: True

nolines

: Turn off lines entirely for generating spectra.
  Default: False

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for solar abundances.
  Either a string corresponding to a built-in table or an array
  of 30 floats corresponding to the abundances of each element
  relative to the abundance of H. Default is set in the SOXS
  configuration file, the default for which is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "feld" : from Feldman U. (1992, Physica Scripta, 46, 202)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)
  "cl17.03" : the abundance table used in Cloudy v17.03.

nei

: If True, use the non-equilibrium ionization tables.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> apec_model = ApecGenerator(0.05, 50.0, 1000, apec_vers="3.0.3",
...                            broadening=True)
```

classes.name: soxs.thermal_spectra.SpexGenerator
documentation:
Create thermal spectral using the SPEX CIE model
(<https://spex-xray.github.io/spex-help/models/cie.html>)
The same underlying machinery as the APEC model is used, as the
SPEX model has been converted to the APEC table format using the
code at <https://github.com/jeremysanders/spex_to_xspec>.

Note that the default abundance table is Anders & Grevasse (1989),
which can be changed using the abund_table keyword argument.

# Parameters

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectral model.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectral model.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectral model.

binscale

: The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
  Default: "linear"

var_elem

: The names of elements to allow to vary freely from the single
  abundance parameter. These must be strings like ["O", "N", "He"].
  Default: None

spex_root

: The directory root where the SPEX model files
  are stored. If not provided, the default is to
  grab them from the tables stored with SOXS.

spex_vers

: The version identifier string for the SPEX files. Default is
  set in the SOXS configuration file, the default for which is
  "3.07.03".

broadening

: Whether the spectral lines should be thermally and velocity
  broadened. Default: True

nolines

: Turn off lines entirely for generating spectra.
  Default: False

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for solar abundances.
  Either a string corresponding to a built-in table or an array
  of 30 floats corresponding to the abundances of each element
  relative to the abundance of H. Default is set in the SOXS
  configuration file, the default for which is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "feld" : from Feldman U. (1992, Physica Scripta, 46, 202)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)
  "cl17.03" : the abundance table used in Cloudy v17.03.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> spex_model = SpexGenerator(0.05, 50.0, 1000, broadening=True)
```

class_variables.name: soxs.thermal_spectra.CloudyCIEGenerator._available_elem
documentation:
Initialize an emission model for a thermal plasma assuming CIE
generated from Cloudy. The sequence of Cloudy commands used
to generate the XSPEC atable are as follows:

#########
title cie_hi_z1
database chianti level MAX
no molecules
no grain physics
set phfit 1996
abundances "./feld.abn"
metals 0.0
hden 0
coronal equil 5 vary
set continuum resolution 0.1
grid range 4.0 to 9.0 in 0.025 dex steps sequential
stop column density 1.5032e+18 linear
iterate to convergence
save xspec atable reflected spectrum "cie_hi_z1.fits" range 0.05 10.
#########

This sequence of commands is repeated for solar and low abundances so that the
abundance parameter can be taken into account via a linear combination of two
tables. For the individual abundances, they are obtained by setting e.g. "element
neon off" in the run and doing the appropriate arithmetic.

The energy range of the generated table at a redshift of 0 is 0.05-10.0 keV. The
default resolution of Cloudy in this range is dE/E = 0.005 for E < 8.16 keV, and
dE/E = 0.03 for higher energies. SOXS provides two different spectral resolutions
for the Cloudy CIE tables, the lower of which is 10 times finer than the above
and the higher is 40 times finer. Which is used can be controlled by the
*model_vers* argument as described below.

Assumes the abundance tables from Feldman 1992.

Parameters
----------
emin : float, (value, unit) tuple, or :class:`~astropy.units.Quantity`
    The minimum energy for the spectral model.
emax : float, (value, unit) tuple, or :class:`~astropy.units.Quantity`
    The maximum energy for the spectral model.
nbins : integer
    The number of bins in the spectral model.
binscale : string, optional
    The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
    Default: "linear"
var_elem : list of strings, optional
    The names of elements to allow to vary freely from the single
    abundance parameter. These must be strings, like ["C", "N", "O"].
    Variable abundances available for the Cloudy CIE model are
    ["C", "N", "O", "Ne", "Mg", "Si", "S", "Ca", "Fe"].
    Default: None
model_vers : string, optional
    The version of the Cloudy CIE tables to use in the calculations.
    Options are:
    "4_lo": Tables computed from Cloudy using a continuum resolution
    of 0.1 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV.
    "4_hi": Tables computed from Cloudy using enhanced continuum
    resolution of 0.025 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV. Excellent
    energy resolution, but may be expensive to evaluate.
    Default: "4_lo"

class_variables.name: soxs.thermal_spectra.IGMGenerator._available_elem
documentation:
Initialize an emission model for a thermal plasma including
photoionization and resonant scattering from the CXB based on
Khabibullin & Churazov 2019
(<https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2019MNRAS.482.4972K/>) and Churazov
et al. 2001 (<https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2001MNRAS.323...93C/>).

The energy range of the generated table at a redshift of 0 is 0.05-10.0 keV. The
default resolution of Cloudy in this range is dE/E = 0.005 for E < 8.16 keV, and
dE/E = 0.03 for higher energies. SOXS provides two different spectral resolutions
for the Cloudy CIE tables, the lower of which is 10 times finer than the above
and the higher is 40 times finer. Which is used can be controlled by the
*model_vers* as described below.

Assumes the abundance tables from Feldman 1992.

# Parameters

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectral model.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectral model.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectral model.

binscale

: The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
  Default: "linear"

resonant_scattering

: Whether or not to include the effects of resonant scattering
  from CXB photons. Default: False

cxb_factor

: The fraction of the CXB photons that are resonant scattered to enhance
  the lines. Default: 0.5

var_elem

: The names of elements to allow to vary freely from the single
  abundance parameter. These must be strings like ["C", "N", "O"].
  Variable abundances available for the IGM model are ["C", "N", "O",
  "Ne", "Mg", "Si", "S", "Ca", "Fe"]. Default: None

model_vers

: The version of the IGM tables to use in the calculations.
  Options are:
  "4_lo": Tables computed from Cloudy using a continuum resolution
  of 0.1 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV.
  "4_hi": Tables computed from Cloudy using enhanced continuum
  resolution of 0.025 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV. Excellent
  energy resolution, but may be expensive to evaluate.
  Default: "4_lo"

  shape you want. Default: 1.0

functions.name: soxs.instrument.generate_events
documentation:
Take unconvolved events and convolve them with instrumental responses. This
function does the following:

1. Determines which events are observed using the ARF
2. Pixelizes the events, applying PSF effects and dithering
3. Determines energy channels using the RMF

This function is not meant to be called by the end-user but is used by
the {func}`~soxs.instrument.instrument_simulator` function.

# Parameters

input_events

: The unconvolved events to be used as input. Can be one of the
  following:
  1\. The name of a SIMPUT catalog file.
  2\. A Python dictionary containing the following items:
  "ra": A NumPy array of right ascension values in degrees.
  "dec": A NumPy array of declination values in degrees.
  "energy": A NumPy array of energy values in keV.
  "flux": The flux of the entire source, in units of erg/cm\*\*2/s.
  3\. A SimputCatalog object.

out_file

: The name of the event file to be written.

exp_time

: The exposure time to use, in seconds.

instrument

: The name of the instrument to use, which picks an instrument
  specification from the instrument registry.

sky_center

: The center RA, Dec coordinates of the observation, in degrees.

no_dither

: If True, turn off dithering entirely. Default: False

dither_params

: The parameters to use to control the size and period of the dither
  pattern. The first two numbers are the dither amplitude in x and y
  detector coordinates in arcseconds, and the second two numbers are
  the dither period in x and y detector coordinates in seconds.
  Default: [8.0, 8.0, 1000.0, 707.0].

roll_angle

: The roll angle of the observation in degrees. Default: 0.0

subpixel_res

: If True, event positions are not randomized within the pixels
  within which they are detected. Default: False

aimpt_shift

: A two-float array-like object which shifts the aimpoint on the
  detector from the nominal position. Units are in arcseconds.
  Default: None, which results in no shift from the nominal aimpoint.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

functions.name: soxs.instrument.make_background
documentation:
Make background events.

# Parameters

exp_time

: The exposure time to use, in seconds.

instrument

: The name of the instrument to use, which picks an instrument
  specification from the instrument registry.

sky_center

: The center RA, Dec coordinates of the observation, in degrees.

foreground

: Whether to include the Galactic foreground. Default: True

instr_bkgnd

: Whether to include the instrumental background. Default: True

no_dither

: If True, turn off dithering entirely. Default: False

dither_params

: The parameters to use to control the size and period of the dither
  pattern. The first two numbers are the dither amplitude in x and y
  detector coordinates in arcseconds, and the second two numbers are
  the dither period in x and y detector coordinates in seconds.
  Default: [8.0, 8.0, 1000.0, 707.0].

ptsrc_bkgnd

: Whether to include the point-source background. Default: True

roll_angle

: The roll angle of the observation in degrees. Default: 0.0

subpixel_res: boolean, optional

: If True, event positions are not randomized within the pixels
  within which they are detected. Default: False

input_pt_sources

: If set to a filename, input the point source positions, fluxes,
  and spectral indices from an ASCII table instead of generating
  them. Default: None

aimpt_shift

: A two-float array-like object which shifts the aimpoint on the
  detector from the nominal position. Units are in arcseconds.
  Default: None, which results in no shift from the nominal aimpoint.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically this will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

: Whether to include the instrumental background. Default: True

ptsrc_bkgnd

: Whether to include the point-source background. Default: True

no_dither

: If True, turn off dithering entirely. Default: False

dither_params

: The parameters to use to control the size and period of the dither
  pattern. The first two numbers are the dither amplitude in x and y
  detector coordinates in arcseconds, and the second two numbers are
  the dither period in x and y detector coordinates in seconds.
  Default: [8.0, 8.0, 1000.0, 707.0].

subpixel_res: boolean, optional

: If True, event positions are not randomized within the pixels
  within which they are detected. Default: False

input_pt_sources

: If set to a filename, input the point source positions, fluxes,
  and spectral indices from an ASCII table instead of generating
  them. Default: None

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

functions.name: soxs.instrument.instrument_simulator
documentation:
Take unconvolved events and create an event file from them. This
function calls generate_events to do the following:

1. Determines which events are observed using the ARF
2. Pixelizes the events, applying PSF effects and dithering
3. Determines energy channels using the RMF

and then calls make_background to add instrumental and astrophysical
backgrounds, unless a background file is provided, in which case
the background events are read from this file. The events are
then written out to a file.

# Parameters

input_events

: The unconvolved events to be used as input. Can be one of the
  following:
  1\. The name of a SIMPUT catalog file.
  2\. A Python dictionary containing the following items:
  "ra": A NumPy array of right ascension values in degrees.
  "dec": A NumPy array of declination values in degrees.
  "energy": A NumPy array of energy values in keV.
  "flux": The flux of the entire source, in units of erg/cm\*\*2/s.

out_file

: The name of the event file to be written.

exp_time

: The exposure time to use, in seconds.

instrument

: The name of the instrument to use, which picks an instrument
  specification from the instrument registry.

sky_center

: The center RA, Dec coordinates of the observation, in degrees.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file with the same name.
  Default: False

instr_bkgnd

: Whether to include the instrumental/particle background.
  Default: True

foreground

: Whether to include the local foreground.
  Default: True

ptsrc_bkgnd

: Whether to include the point-source background.
  Default: True

bkgnd_file

: If set, backgrounds will be loaded from this file and not generated
  on the fly. Default: None

no_dither

: If True, turn off dithering entirely. Default: False

dither_params

: The parameters to use to control the size and period of the dither
  pattern. The first two numbers are the dither amplitude in x and y
  detector coordinates in arcseconds, and the second two numbers are
  the dither period in x and y detector coordinates in seconds.
  Default: [8.0, 8.0, 1000.0, 707.0].

roll_angle

: The roll angle of the observation in degrees. Default: 0.0

subpixel_res: boolean, optional

: If True, event positions are not randomized within the pixels
  within which they are detected. Default: False

aimpt_shift

: A two-float array-like object which shifts the aimpoint on the
  detector from the nominal position. Units are in arcseconds.
  Default: None, which results in no shift from the nominal aimpoint.

input_pt_sources

: If set to a filename, input the point source positions, fluxes,
  and spectral indices from an ASCII table instead of generating
  them. Default: None

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> instrument_simulator("sloshing_simput.fits", "sloshing_evt.fits",
...                      300000.0, "lynx_hdxi", [30., 45.], overwrite=True)
```

functions.name: soxs.instrument.simulate_spectrum
documentation:
Generate a PI or PHA spectrum from a {class}`~soxs.spectra.Spectrum`
by convolving it with responses. To be used if one wants to
create a spectrum without worrying about spatial response. Similar
to XSPEC's "fakeit".

# Parameters

spec

: The spectrum to be convolved. If None is supplied, only backgrounds
  will be simulated (if they are turned on).

instrument

: Two options:
  1\. The name of the instrument to use, which picks an instrument
  specification from the instrument registry.
  2\. A 2-tuple specifying an ARF, RMF pair, or a 3-tuple specifying
  an ARF, RMF, and a background spectrum specification, where the
  latter is a 2-element list giving the name of the background
  spectrum file and the normalization of the background spectrum in
  square arcminutes. If the ARF is set to None, a flat ARF will be
  assumed with a value of 1.0 cm\*\*2. This may be useful for simulating
  particle backgrounds.

exp_time

: The exposure time in seconds.

out_file

: The file to write the spectrum to.

instr_bkgnd

: Whether to include the instrumental/particle background.
  Default: False

foreground

: Whether to include the local foreground.
  Default: False

ptsrc_bkgnd

: Whether to include the unresolved point-source background.
  Default: False

bkgnd_area

: The area on the sky for the background components, in square arcminutes.
  Default: None, necessary to specify if any of the background components
  are turned on.

noisy

: If False, simulate_spectrum will not use counting (Poisson) statistics
  when creating the spectrum. If any backgrounds are on, this must be
  set to True. Default: True

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing file. Default: False

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> spec = soxs.Spectrum.from_file("my_spectrum.txt")
>>> soxs.simulate_spectrum(spec, "lynx_lxm", 100000.0,
...                        "my_spec.pi", overwrite=True)
```

functions.name: soxs.cosmology.make_cosmological_sources
documentation:
Make an X-ray source made up of contributions from
galaxy clusters, galaxy groups, and galaxies.

# Parameters

exp_time

: The exposure time of the observation in seconds.

fov

: The field of view in arcminutes.

sky_center

: The center RA, Dec of the field of view in degrees.

cat_center

: The center of the field in the coordinates of the
  halo catalog, which range from -5.0 to 5.0 in
  degrees in both directions. If None is given, a
  center will be randomly chosen.

absorb_model

: The absorption model to use, "wabs" or "tbabs". Default: "wabs"

nH

: The hydrogen column in units of 10\*\*22 atoms/cm\*\*2.
  Default: 0.05

area

: The effective area in cm\*\*2. It must be large enough
  so that a sufficiently large sample is drawn for the
  ARF. Default: 40000.

output_sources

: If set to a filename, output the properties of the sources
  within the field of view to a file. Default: None

write_regions

: If set to a filename, output circle ds9 regions corresponding to the
  positions of the halos with radii corresponding to their R500
  projected on the sky. Default: None

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

functions.name: soxs.cosmology.make_cosmological_sources_file
documentation:
Make a SIMPUT catalog made up of contributions from
galaxy clusters, galaxy groups, and galaxies.

# Parameters

filename

: The filename for the SIMPUT catalog.

name

: The name of the SIMPUT photon list.

exp_time

: The exposure time of the observation in seconds.

fov

: The field of view in arcminutes.

sky_center

: The center RA, Dec of the field of view in degrees.

cat_center

: The center of the field in the coordinates of the
  halo catalog, which range from -5.0 to 5.0 degrees
  along both axes. If None is given, a center will be
  randomly chosen.

absorb_model

: The absorption model to use, "wabs" or "tbabs". Default: "wabs"

nH

: The hydrogen column in units of 10\*\*22 atoms/cm\*\*2.
  Default: 0.05

area

: The effective area in cm\*\*2. It must be large enough
  so that a sufficiently large sample is drawn for the
  ARF. Default: 40000.

overwrite

: Set to True to overwrite previous files. Default: False

output_sources

: If set to a filename, output the properties of the sources
  within the field of view to an ASCII file. Default: None

write_regions

: If set to a filename, output circle ds9 regions corresponding to the
  positions of the halos with radii corresponding to their R500
  projected on the sky. Default: None

src_filename

: If set, this will be the filename to write the source
  to. By default, the source will be written to the same
  file as the SIMPUT catalog

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

append

: If True, the photon list source will be appended to an existing
  SIMPUT catalog. Default: False

    The filename to write the file to.
overwrite : boolean, optional
    Whether to overwrite an existing
    file with the same name. Default: False

methods.name: soxs.spectra.Spectrum.apply_foreground_absorption
documentation:
Given a hydrogen column density, apply
galactic foreground absorption to the spectrum.

# Parameters

nH

: The hydrogen column in units of 10\*\*22 atoms/cm\*\*2

model

: The model for absorption to use. Options are "wabs"
  (Wisconsin, Morrison and McCammon; ApJ 270, 119) or
  "tbabs" (Tuebingen-Boulder, Wilms, J., Allen, A., &
  McCray, R. 2000, ApJ, 542, 914). Default: "wabs".

redshift

: The redshift of the absorbing material. Default: 0.0

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for abundances in the
  absorption model. Default is set in the SOXS
  configuration file, the default for which is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "feld" : from Feldman U. (1992, Physica Scripta, 46, 202)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)
  "cl17.03" : the default abundance table in Cloudy 17.03

methods.name: soxs.spectra.Spectrum.add_emission_line
documentation:
Add an emission line to this spectrum.

# Parameters

line_center

: The line center position in units of keV, in the observer frame.

line_width

: The line width (FWHM) in units of keV, in the observer frame. Can also
  input the line width in units of velocity in the rest frame. For the Voigt
  profile, a list, tuple, or array of two values should be provided since there
  are two line widths, the Lorentzian and the Gaussian (in that order).

line_amp

: The integrated line amplitude in the units of the flux

line_type

: The line profile type. Default: "gaussian"

methods.name: soxs.spectra.Spectrum.add_absorption_line
documentation:
Add an absorption line to this spectrum.

# Parameters

line_center

: The line center position in units of keV, in the observer frame.

line_width

: The line width (FWHM) in units of keV, in the observer frame. Can also
  input the line width in units of velocity in the rest frame. For the Voigt
  profile, a list, tuple, or array of two values should be provided since there
  are two line widths, the Lorentzian and the Gaussian (in that order).

equiv_width

: The equivalent width of the line, in units of milli-Angstrom

line_type

: The line profile type. Default: "gaussian"

methods.name: soxs.spectra.Spectrum.generate_energies
documentation:
Generate photon energies from this spectrum
given an exposure time and effective area.

# Parameters

t_exp

: The exposure time in seconds.

area

: The effective area in cm\*\*2. If one is creating
  events for a SIMPUT file, a constant should be
  used, and it must be large enough so that a
  sufficiently large sample is drawn for the ARF.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. This will typically only
  be specified if you have a reason to generate the same
  set of random numbers, such as for a test. Default is None,
  which sets the seed based on the system time.

quiet

: If True, log messages will not be displayed when
  creating energies. Useful if you have to loop over
  a lot of spectra. Default: False

methods.name: soxs.spectra.Spectrum.plot
documentation:
Make a quick Matplotlib plot of the spectrum. A Matplotlib
figure and axis is returned.

# Parameters

lw

: The width of the lines in the plots. Default: 2.0 px.

xmin

: The left-most energy in keV to plot. Default is the
  minimum value in the spectrum.

xmax

: The right-most energy in keV to plot. Default is the
  maximum value in the spectrum.

ymin

: The lower extent of the y-axis. By default, it is set automatically.

ymax

: The upper extent of the y-axis. By default, it is set automatically.

xscale

: The scaling of the x-axis of the plot. Default: "log"

yscale

: The scaling of the y-axis of the plot. Default: "log"

label

: The label of the spectrum. Default: None

fontsize

: Font size for labels and axes. Default: 18

fig

: A Figure instance to plot in. Default: None, one will be
  created if not provided.

ax

: An Axes instance to plot in. Default: None, one will be
  created if not provided.

# Returns

A tuple of the {class}`~matplotlib.figure.Figure` and the {class}`~matplotlib.axes.Axes` objects.

methods.name: soxs.spectra.ConvolvedSpectrum.convolve
documentation:
Generate a convolved spectrum by convolving a spectrum with an
ARF.

# Parameters

spectrum

: The input spectrum to convolve with.

arf

: The ARF to use in the convolution.

use_arf_energies

: If True, use the energy binning of the ARF for
  the convolved spectrum. Default: False, which uses
  the original spectral binning.
